purpose: Define which product and platform capabilities require a human-usable backend control panel surface, production gate mapping, and machine evidence.
productionControlRule: Any capability that can affect launch, revenue, fulfillment, customer access, security, cost, provider state, data, backup, rollback, compliance, or a human production decision must appear in the backend control panel and must map to an executable gate or explicit evidence field before production promotion.

admissionCriteria.title: External provider or account
admissionCriteria.description: Provider credentials, endpoint health, account status, or webhook state can affect product behavior.

admissionCriteria.title: Money, cost, or quota
admissionCriteria.description: The capability can spend money, block revenue, consume quota, or change customer-visible billing outcomes.

admissionCriteria.title: Customer, order, or delivery impact
admissionCriteria.description: The capability can change checkout, fulfillment, access recovery, support, or customer-visible content.

admissionCriteria.title: Queue, retry, or background work
admissionCriteria.description: Operators need visibility into pending, failed, replayable, or review-required work.

admissionCriteria.title: Human review or approval
admissionCriteria.description: The capability can create drafts, decisions, or artifacts that should be accepted, edited, rejected, or audited by a human.

admissionCriteria.title: Site-specific configuration
admissionCriteria.description: Behavior differs by site, tenant, sales channel, product type, or deployment environment.

admissionCriteria.title: Security, audit, or compliance
admissionCriteria.description: The capability handles secrets, sensitive data, provider access, risk signals, or compliance-relevant records.

admissionCriteria.title: Production gate or launch decision
admissionCriteria.description: The capability contributes to staging, production promotion, go-live, rollback, or irreversible runtime decisions.

admissionCriteria.title: Runtime evidence
admissionCriteria.description: The capability needs real runtime proof, smoke checks, provider probes, or generated evidence before it is safe to rely on.

requiredSurface.title: Status
requiredSurface.description: Show enabled state, readiness, health, recent failures, and missing configuration without exposing secrets.

requiredSurface.title: Configuration
requiredSurface.description: Show provider codes, endpoint references, model or handler choices, site scope, and secret references.

requiredSurface.title: Queues
requiredSurface.description: Show pending, failed, retryable, and human-review items with timestamps and ownership.

requiredSurface.title: Operator actions
requiredSurface.description: Provide only reversible or auditable actions unless a decision gate explicitly requires human approval.

requiredSurface.title: Auditability
requiredSurface.description: Expose recent activity, actor context, artifact references, and links to audit logs where available.

requiredSurface.title: Evidence
requiredSurface.description: Expose the command, timestamp, result, report path, or evidence reference used by production gates.

requiredSurface.title: Production gates
requiredSurface.description: Show which site lifecycle controls and evidence fields block promotion.

requiredSurface.title: Safe empty state
requiredSurface.description: Empty or disabled states must still explain readiness in operational terms and avoid pretending the capability is healthy.

forbiddenSurface.title: Plaintext secrets
forbiddenSurface.description: Never render API secrets, database URLs, Redis URLs, private keys, certificate private keys, or provider tokens.

forbiddenSurface.title: Arbitrary command execution
forbiddenSurface.description: Never expose a free-form shell command input in the backend panel.

forbiddenSurface.title: Unguarded irreversible action
forbiddenSurface.description: Never allow go-live, rollback, DNS mutation, WAF mutation, Access mutation, data migration, or production write enablement without an explicit human gate and machine evidence.

forbiddenSurface.title: Human attestation as proof
forbiddenSurface.description: Human confirmation is a decision input only; it must not replace executable runtime evidence.

requiredProductionSurfaces.title: WAF, Access, origin, and rate limits
